metadata.name: Handoff
metadata.description: Produces a structured, machine-readable output document at the end of every workflow execution. This document enables the Director to chain workflows together, track cumulative costs, propagate errors, and suggest next actions. The handoff format is YAML for readability and ease of parsing by both humans and downstream workflows.

triggers.trigger: When a workflow fails and cannot recover.
When a workflow completes partially (some steps succeeded, others failed).

consumers.consumer: Downstream workflows that depend on this workflow's outputs.
The cost tracking system for cumulative budget monitoring.

output_format.description: The canonical handoff document structure. Every workflow execution produces exactly one handoff document in this format.

field.name: workflow_id
field.description: Unique identifier for this workflow execution instance. Format: `{workflow_name}-{timestamp}-{short_hash}` Example: `generate-episode-20260301T143022-a7f3`

field.name: workflow_name
field.description: The name of the workflow that was executed. Example: `generate-episode`, `build-character-refs`, `render-scene`

field.name: status
field.description: The final status of the workflow execution.

values.value: All steps completed without errors. All outputs are available.
The workflow encountered an unrecoverable error and halted. Some outputs may be missing. The `errors` array contains details.
Some steps completed successfully but others failed or were skipped. The `outputs` array indicates which outputs are available. The `errors` array indicates which steps failed.

field.name: started_at
field.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the workflow began execution.

field.name: completed_at
field.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the workflow finished (success, fail, or partial).

field.name: duration_seconds
field.description: Total wall-clock time for the workflow execution in seconds.

field.name: outputs
field.description: List of all outputs produced by the workflow. Each output entry identifies what was produced, where it is stored, and its status.

item_schema.field: Absolute path to the output file on disk.
The step ID that generated this output.
SHA-256 hash of the output file for integrity verification.
Additional output-specific metadata (resolution, duration, frame count, etc.).

field.name: errors
field.description: List of all errors encountered during execution. Empty array if status is `success`. Each error entry provides enough detail for diagnosis and potential automated recovery.

item_schema.field: Error classification matching error-recovery.xml types: missing_file, unresolved_variable, validation_failure, api_failure, cost_exceeded, asset_quality_fail.
The workflow step where the error occurred.
Structured error details for automated processing.
Outcome of recovery attempt: resolved, failed, skipped.
ISO 8601 timestamp of when the error occurred.

field.name: cost_summary
field.description: Breakdown of all costs incurred during workflow execution. Used by the Director for cumulative budget tracking and by the cost-gate protocol for threshold monitoring.

sub_fields.field: Total cost in USD for this workflow run.

item_schema.field: The tool or API used (e.g., gpt-4o, dall-e-3, runway-gen4).

sub_fields.field: Aggregated cost per tool across all steps. Keys are tool names, values are total cost in USD for that tool.
Running total of all costs in the current production session (across all workflows since the session started).
Remaining budget based on the configured budget limit. Null if no budget limit is configured.
Whether any retake buffer was consumed during this workflow.

field.name: next_suggested
field.description: Suggestion for the Director on what workflow to run next. Based on the workflow's own knowledge of the production pipeline. The Director may override this suggestion.

sub_fields.field: Name of the suggested next workflow.
Why this workflow is suggested next.
Suggested parameters to pass to the next workflow, typically derived from this workflow's outputs.
Whether this suggestion blocks other workflows from running. If true, the Director should run this next before anything else.

field.name: production_state_update
field.description: Records which production_state flags were set and any episode status transition that occurred during FINAL-4 (Update Production State). Present only when the workflow declares production_state_flags and completes with status "success". Used for auditability and by the Director to track cumulative production progress.

sub_fields.field: List of production_state flag names that were set to true. Example: ["script_finalized"]

field.name: status_transition
field.field: New episode status value. Same as "from" if no transition occurred.

sub_fields.field: The episode ID whose production_state was updated.

field.name: context_carry
field.description: Key-value pairs from the workflow context that should be carried forward to downstream workflows. This is the mechanism for passing state between chained workflows without relying on file I/O.

procedure.description: Steps the workflow engine follows to generate the handoff document at the conclusion of a workflow.

step.name: Determine Final Status
step.action: Evaluate all step results: - If all steps passed → status = "success" - If any step failed and was not recovered → check if other steps succeeded: - If some succeeded → status = "partial" - If none succeeded or the failure was in a critical path → status = "failed"

step.name: Collect Outputs
step.action: Iterate through all steps that declared outputs. For each output: - Verify the output file exists on disk. - Compute the checksum. - Record the file size. - Set status to "available" if it exists, "missing" if the step was skipped, or "failed" if the step errored.

step.name: Collect Errors
step.action: Aggregate all errors from the workflow execution log. For each error: - Classify it according to error-recovery.xml types. - Include the recovery attempt result if recovery was tried. - Preserve the full error detail for debugging.

step.name: Calculate Cost Summary
step.action: Sum all cost entries recorded during step execution. Break down by step, by tool, and compute the cumulative session total. Check remaining budget against the configured limit.

step.name: Determine Next Suggestion
step.action: Based on the workflow definition's `on_complete` block: - If status is "success" → use the `on_success.next` declaration. - If status is "partial" → use `on_partial.next` if defined, else suggest re-running failed steps. - If status is "failed" → use `on_failure.next` if defined, else suggest manual intervention. Populate the params from the current workflow's outputs and context.

step.name: Build Context Carry
step.action: Extract variables from the workflow context that are marked with `carry_forward: true` in the workflow definition. Include them in the context_carry field for downstream consumption.

step.name: Write Handoff Document
step.action: Serialize the complete handoff structure to YAML. Write it to: `{episode_path}/.cookie/handoffs/{workflow_id}.yaml` Also store it in the workflow context for immediate Director consumption.

step.name: Signal Director
step.action: Emit a `workflow.completed` event with the handoff document attached. The Director receives this event and proceeds with routing based on the handoff contents.

example.description: Example handoff document for a completed scene rendering workflow.
example.yaml_content:
workflow_id: "render-scene-20260301T143022-a7f3" workflow_name: "render-scene" status: "success" started_at: "2026-03-01T14:30:22Z" completed_at: "2026-03-01T14:35:47Z" duration_seconds: 325
outputs: - name: "scene_03_video" type: "video/mp4" path: "/projects/my-series/episodes/ep01/output/scenes/scene-03.mp4" status: "available" produced_by_step: "merge-video" checksum: "sha256:a1b2c3d4e5f6..." size_bytes: 15728640 metadata: resolution: "1920x1080" duration_seconds: 12.5 fps: 24 codec: "h264"
- name: "scene_03_narration" type: "audio/mp3" path: "/projects/my-series/episodes/ep01/output/audio/scene-03-narration.mp3" status: "available" produced_by_step: "generate-narration" checksum: "sha256:f6e5d4c3b2a1..." size_bytes: 524288 metadata: duration_seconds: 12.5 sample_rate: 44100
errors: []
cost_summary: total_cost_usd: 2.35 breakdown_by_step: - step_id: "generate-frames" tool: "dall-e-3" cost_usd: 1.20 units: "images" quantity: 3 - step_id: "generate-video" tool: "runway-gen4" cost_usd: 0.80 units: "seconds" quantity: 12 - step_id: "generate-narration" tool: "elevenlabs" cost_usd: 0.35 units: "characters" quantity: 450 breakdown_by_tool: dall-e-3: 1.20 runway-gen4: 0.80 elevenlabs: 0.35 cumulative_session_cost_usd: 14.80 budget_remaining_usd: 35.20 retake_buffer_used: false
next_suggested: workflow_name: "render-scene" reason: "Next scene in sequence (scene 04 of 8)" params: episode_id: "ep01" scene_id: "04" priority: "required" blocking: false
context_carry: episode_id: "ep01" scenes_completed: ["01", "02", "03"] scenes_remaining: ["04", "05", "06", "07", "08"] cumulative_cost: 14.80

parseability.description: Requirements for ensuring the handoff document is reliably parseable by downstream consumers.

requirements.requirement: The handoff YAML must be valid YAML 1.2. No YAML aliases or anchors are permitted to ensure flat, self-contained documents.
All string values that could be confused with YAML types (true, false, null, numbers) must be explicitly quoted.
All file paths must be absolute. No relative paths are permitted in the handoff document.
The `errors` array must always be present, even if empty. Downstream consumers must not need to null-check this field.
The `outputs` array must always be present, even if empty.
The `cost_summary` object must always be present with at minimum `total_cost_usd` set (0.0 if no costs were incurred).
Timestamps must always be in UTC with the `Z` suffix. No local time offsets are permitted.
The handoff document must not exceed 1MB in size. If output metadata is large, reference external files rather than inlining content.

retention.description: Rules for how long handoff documents are retained and when they can be cleaned up.

rules.rule: Handoff documents are retained for 30 days after creation.
Handoff documents for failed workflows are retained for 90 days to support post-mortem analysis.
Cleanup is triggered by the `cleanup-handoffs` maintenance workflow or manually by the user via the Director.
Before deletion, handoff documents are archived to a compressed log at `{project-root}/.cookie/handoff-archive.log.gz`.
